campaign_goal: Educational audience development for Nature & Culture public beta.

videos.title: World Heritage: Machu Picchu in 3 Minutes
script_outline: Hook: why Machu Picchu matters to public memory.
Context: introduce source family UNESCO World Heritage and country/region Peru.
Learning beat: explain one visual clue and one historical or conservation insight.
Audience bridge: connect the subject to Nature & Culture collections, series, and shop validation.
Close: invite viewers to explore the public beta.
narration_outline: Clear educational explanation with source-aware language.

segments.title: 25 places that shaped world memory
segments.hook: What makes a place unforgettable across cultures and centuries?
outline: Open with Machu Picchu, Giza, the Great Wall, and Taj Mahal as global recognition anchors.
Explain how heritage sites become learning objects, not just travel images.
Show posters, puzzles, calendars, and books as validation formats.
Close on stewardship, access, and no-payment validation.
segments.CTA: Explore the RC12 heritage showcase portfolio.

segments.title: Flagship species as conservation stories
segments.hook: A lion, tiger, panda, reef, and polar bear can teach more than recognition alone.
outline: Introduce flagship species from the showcase assets and products.
Connect biodiversity, climate, and family learning.
Show conservation posters, puzzles, calendars, and books.
Invite viewers to compare species stories and classroom uses.

segments.title: Building a balanced global showcase collection
segments.hook: A strong collection needs heritage, biodiversity, geography, culture, and climate.
outline: Walk through the top showcase collections and their balance tags.
Explain why public-domain art, maps, science, and species complement UNESCO icons.
Show launch-ready collection boards for Pinterest and email.
Close with the first collection themes for validation.

segments.title: From story to product series
segments.hook: The best products come from series with narrative strength, educational strength, and product potential.
outline: Introduce Seven Wonders, World Heritage Travel Posters, Big Cats, Historic Maps, and Space Mission series.
Explain the three scoring dimensions used in RC11.
Show how a series becomes posters, prints, puzzles, calendars, and books.
segments.CTA: Review the top RC12 launch series.
